 {"id":"wa-1ez8","title":"Set up automated nightly rebase CI for WezTerm fork","description":"## Overview

WezTerm is updated nightly by its author. To maintain our feature branch, we need automated rebasing with conflict detection and alerting.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: wa-20fw (WezTerm fork with wa integration)

## CI Workflow Design

### GitHub Actions Workflow

\`\`\`.github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml\`\`\`
\`\`\`yaml
name: Rebase on Upstream WezTerm

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * *'  # Daily at 6 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:      # Manual trigger

jobs:
  rebase:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: feature/wa-integration
          token: \${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "wa-bot"
          git config user.email "wa-bot@example.com"
      
      - name: Add Upstream Remote
        run: |
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
          git fetch upstream main
      
      - name: Attempt Rebase
        id: rebase
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          git rebase upstream/main
          echo "rebase_status=\$?" >> \$GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Push if Successful
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          git push --force-with-lease origin feature/wa-integration
      
      - name: Create Conflict Issue
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'failure'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const { data: issues } = await github.rest.issues.listForRepo({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              labels: 'rebase-conflict',
              state: 'open'
            });
            
            if (issues.length === 0) {
              await github.rest.issues.create({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                title: 'Rebase conflict with upstream WezTerm',
                body: \`The nightly rebase on upstream WezTerm failed due to conflicts.
                
                **Action Required**: Manual resolution needed.
                
                \\\`\\\`\\\`bash
                git fetch upstream main
                git rebase upstream/main
                # Resolve conflicts
                git rebase --continue
                git push --force-with-lease
                \\\`\\\`\\\`
                \`,
                labels: ['rebase-conflict', 'urgent']
              });
            }
      
      - name: Run Tests After Rebase
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          cargo build --features wa-integration
          cargo test --features wa-integration

  notify:
    needs: rebase
    if: failure()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Send Notification
        run: |
          # Notify via email, Slack, Discord, etc.
          echo "Rebase failed - notification would be sent"
\`\`\`

### Handling Conflicts

When conflicts occur:
1. CI creates GitHub issue with "rebase-conflict" label
2. Human/agent reviews conflicting files
3. Usually conflicts are in files we modified (minimal set)
4. Resolve, test, push

### Minimizing Conflicts

To reduce conflict frequency:
1. Keep wa changes behind \`#[cfg(feature)]\` — no logic changes to upstream code
2. Use separate files where possible (mux/src/wa_events.rs)
3. Add wa code at end of functions, not middle
4. Avoid reformatting upstream code

## Alternative: Patch-Based Approach

Instead of maintaining a branch, maintain a patch series:

\`\`\`bash
# Generate patches
git format-patch upstream/main..feature/wa-integration -o patches/

# Apply patches to fresh clone
git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
cd wezterm
git am ../patches/*.patch
\`\`\`

Patches are more explicit about what we change, easier to review.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow created
- [ ] Nightly rebase runs automatically
- [ ] Conflicts create GitHub issues
- [ ] Notifications configured
- [ ] Post-rebase tests run
- [ ] Documentation for manual conflict resolution
- [ ] 30-day trial period: track conflict frequency

## Files to Create

- .github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml
- docs/maintaining-wezterm-fork.md

## References

- GitHub Actions: https://docs.github.com/en/actions
- Git rebase: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-rebase
- WezTerm release cadence: nightly builds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:53.398103664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:05.800522Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:52:05.800499Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1ez8","depends_on_id":"wa-20fw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-1ez8","depends_on_id":"wa-2xe4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1f4u","title":"FTUI-08.4 Resilience/chaos validation (resize storms, output bursts, failure injection)","description":"## Background\nReal-world operator sessions include abrupt resizing, noisy outputs, and transient failures.\n\n## Deliverables\n- resilience test scenarios and artifacts\n- failure-injection hooks for lifecycle and command handoff paths\n- stability report tied to rollout criteria\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- system remains usable under stress scenarios\n- failure behavior is bounded and diagnosable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmLynx","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:10:27.440125802Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:29:07.896699655Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:29:07.896557862Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1f4u","depends_on_id":"wa-1kut","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:10:27.516971529Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1f4u","depends_on_id":"wa-3gii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:23:31.509813125Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1f4u","depends_on_id":"wa-1p3f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:23:36.234994887Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1hbj","title":"FTUI-05.1 Build base ftui app shell (tabs, layout, global status, view router)","description":"## Background\nAll view migrations depend on a stable shell runtime.\n\n## Deliverables\n- top-level ftui app scaffolding\n- view router + tab navigation surface\n- shared status/footer/error banner regions\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- shell hosts all target views\n- global nav semantics are defined and tested.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:07.004777535Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:13:41.224829807Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:13:41.224765858Z","close_reason":"FTUI-05.1 complete: WaModel implementing ftui::Model with init/update/view cycle, View enum with tab navigation, global keybindings, CellStyle abstraction, rendering helpers, run_tui entry point, 7 unit tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1hbj","depends_on_id":"wa-38vw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:07.017745Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1hbj","depends_on_id":"wa-3cso","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:20.038854745Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1hbj","depends_on_id":"wa-3kal","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:29.52870044Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1hoj","title":"Implement quiescence detector (queue drained + quiet window)","description":"# Task: Implement quiescence detector

## Goal
Implement a deterministic quiescence detector for "the system is done".

## Requirements
- Signals (minimum viable):
  - ingest queue depth
  - storage writer queue depth
  - last segment/event timestamp (monotonic if possible)
- Quiescence definition:
  - all queues drained
  - no new segments/events for a configurable quiet window
- Provide a query API suitable for:
  - E2E harness
  - diagnostics
  - potential production "drain" operations

## Testing
- Integration tests simulate slow consumers and validate:
  - quiescence eventually achieved when producer stops
  - quiescence is not reported while queues still drain

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scripts can block on quiescence without wall-clock sleeps.
- Timeout errors are actionable (include queue depths and last timestamps).

 {"id":"wa-27hp","title":"Per-pane tailers with adaptive polling + concurrency limits","description":"# Task: Per-pane tailers with adaptive polling

## Goal
For each observed pane, run a tailer that produces PaneDelta segments with low overhead.

This task is responsible for the "poll WezTerm" loop and ensuring the system remains performant.

## Privacy / selection
- Tailers must run ONLY for observed panes.
- Ignored panes (pane selection filters) must never be tailed.

## Requirements
- Adaptive polling:
  - fast when output is changing
  - slow when idle
- Concurrency limits:
  - avoid running too many simultaneous `wezterm cli get-text` calls
- Backpressure:
  - if downstream queues are full, do not explode memory

## Deliverables
- Adaptive polling policy (global + per-pane priority).
- Parallel processing limiter (semaphore).
- Tailer supervisor:
  - start tailer on new observed pane
  - stop on pane closure
  - stop/start on observation decision changes (observed <-> ignored)

## Logging
- Log when:
  - a tailer starts/stops
  - a pane is ignored (and why)
  - backpressure forces slow-down
- Never log pane content.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests:
  - ignored panes never start tailers
  - adaptive polling increases/decreases interval as expected
  - concurrency limits cap simultaneous in-flight get-text calls
  - backpressure causes slowdown rather than unbounded buffering

## Acceptance Criteria
- With many panes, CPU stays low when idle.
- Active panes are polled frequently enough to keep latency low.
- Ignored panes are never polled.
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:56:42.507839132Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.294486-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:06:56.400477552Z"}
 {"id":"wa-27hy6","title":"Competing build coordination — shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR and build locks","description":"# Competing Build Coordination

## Skills: /idea-wizard, /extreme-software-optimization

## Problem
5+ agents targeting the same Rust project = 5 independent cargo builds, each cold-starting its build cache. On a 64-core machine this causes:
- 5× the disk I/O (compiling the same crate 5 times)
- Build cache thrashing (no shared incremental artifacts)
- Can consume 100%+ CPU for hours

Detection: `ps aux | grep cc1plus | grep -oP "target[^/]*/" | sort | uniq -c`

## Solution

### 1. Shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR per project
All agents working on the same project share one target directory:
- Set via environment variable injection in pane launch
- Use file locking (flock on Linux, fcntl on macOS) to prevent concurrent writes to same compilation unit

### 2. Build coordinator
- Detect when agent is about to run cargo build/check/test
- Check if another agent build is already running for same project
- If so: wait for it to complete, then reuse artifacts
- Coordinate via FrankenTerm event bus (which will be lock-free per wa-1sm78)

### 3. Build cache warming
- On first agent launch for a project, trigger a background cargo check
- All subsequent agents benefit from warm cache

### 4. sccache integration (optional)
- If sccache is available, configure as RUSTC_WRAPPER automatically
- Shared compilation cache across all panes

## Tests
- 5 agents build same project, verify only 1 actual compilation
- Measure disk I/O reduction (should be ~80% less)
- Verify file locking prevents corruption (test on both Linux and macOS)
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Measure coordination overhead per build request (<5ms)
- **Stress test**: 20 agents competing for same project, verify no deadlock

## Acceptance criteria
- Shared target directory configured automatically
- Competing builds detected and coordinated
- Build time for 2nd+ agent reduced by >70%
- No build cache corruption under concurrent access
- Works on both Linux (flock) and macOS (fcntl)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:11:37.866997Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:37:15.701281-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T23:37:15.701281-05:00","close_reason":"Build coordination module (build_coord.rs) complete: BuildLock with advisory file locking, BuildCoordConfig, BuildEnv for shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR + sccache detection, find_project_root(), detect_cargo_command(), check_build_running(). 16 tests all passing."}
 {"id":"wa-27vq","title":"TUI transcript/search: query FTS, view snippets, open around match","description":"# Task: TUI transcript/search

## Goal
Allow interactive transcript search so a human can answer:
- “Where did the agent say X?”
- “What happened right before the failure?”

This is a TUI wrapper around the same FTS-backed search used by `wa query` / `wa robot search`.

## UX requirements
- Query entry:
  - input box for FTS query string
  - optional scoping controls (pane/domain/time)

- Results list:
  - show matches with redacted snippets + timestamps
  - keyboard navigation (up/down/page)

- Match inspection:
  - select a match to view more surrounding context (still redacted)
  - allow “open around match” by fetching a larger window via the query layer

## Safety
- No raw output bytes should be displayed unless they have passed the same redaction pipeline as CLI/robot.
- Avoid allowing “query injection” into SQL: the query layer must remain parameterized and treat the FTS query carefully.

## Testing strategy
- Unit tests (feature `tui`) with a fake `QueryClient`:
  - query submission → result rendering
  - scoping controls propagate correctly
  - paging/selection state transitions

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can enter a query and see a list of matches.
- Selecting a match shows a larger redacted context window quickly.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:30:55.771857861Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.199541-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:33:59.731168066Z"}
